rationale: In the General Discussion, the authors explicitly identify the cited paper as representative of phantom-option research and summarize what that stream examined. They then position their own contribution as going beyond that scope by studying aspirant desirability, pre-existing incumbents, and underlying cognitive-affective processes. This citation therefore serves conceptual framing and contribution positioning rather than a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Hedgcock, Rao, and Chen (2009) as prior phantom-option/decoy-dynamics work and extends that line to a different focal outcome: post-jilt shifts away from incumbent options. It adds a two-phase process account and affective mechanism emphasis beyond the attraction-effect focus associated with the cited work.
